name: Ionizing Radiation
definition: Ionizing radiation is an exposure category that can damage DNA and influence cancer risk at the population level. GENARCH includes this exposure as an educational context for gene–environment interaction discussions; it does not provide clinical recommendations.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Ionizing radiation can induce DNA breaks and activate repair pathways; cancer risk effects depend on dose, timing, and tissue context.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Developing tissues can be more sensitive to mutagenic exposures in some study contexts.

references.title: Ionizing radiation and cancer risk: an overview (review)
